quiz1.question: Apple was 90 days from bankruptcy when Steve Jobs returned. How long until profitability?
quiz1.explanation: Jobs cut products, secured a Microsoft investment, and refocused Apple — back in the black within a year.

quiz2.question: WeWork hit a $47B valuation before its IPO. Roughly what was it worth months later?
quiz2.explanation: After the S-1 leaked, investors saw the losses. The IPO collapsed and the valuation cratered — a classic rise-and-fall story.

steve-jobs.headline: What Apple hid about Jobs' return
steve-jobs.record: Steve Jobs — co-founder. Ousted in 1985. Returned as interim CEO in 1997.
steve-jobs.real: The board offered him the job in a parking lot conversation. His first move wasn't a product — it was killing almost every project Apple had.

elon-musk.headline: What Tesla hid about Christmas Eve 2008
elon-musk.record: Elon Musk — CEO of Tesla and SpaceX. Nearly out of cash in late 2008.
elon-musk.real: Both companies were days from death. He bet his last personal dollars on one more Falcon 1 launch — and a Daimler deal that saved Tesla.

ferrari.headline: What Enzo hid about the Ford deal
ferrari.record: Enzo Ferrari — founder. Nearly sold Ferrari to Ford in 1963.
ferrari.real: He walked away over a clause about racing control. Ford's rage funded the GT40 program — and Ferrari's myth grew sharper.

default.headline: What the boardroom minutes never said
default.record: The official story — polished for investors and the press.
default.real: The real decisions happened in late-night calls, leaked decks, and bets nobody wanted on the record.

0.title: I love this app!
0.body: I love this app! I thought I knew everything about Steve Jobs. Turns out the fight to get back into Apple was far more brutal than any documentary showed — and the panels make it hit harder.

1.title: The perfect way to unwind after a chaotic day
1.body: The founder stories pull you straight into another world. It's sharp, cinematic, and surprisingly moving. Every chapter leaves me wanting just one more — without a three-hour podcast.

2.title: I downloaded this expecting a quick read
2.body: I downloaded this expecting a quick escape, but I got hooked on the FTX saga almost immediately. It feels like Succession, but with real balance sheets and much higher stakes.
